own_review.point: updateSharedConfig() now persists every property not prefixed fluo.connection.* into Zookeeper CONFIG_SHARED — this includes fluo.accumulo.password and fluo.accumulo.user (required by hasRequiredAdminProps and present in fluo-app.properties). Plaintext Accumulo credentials end up stored in ZK and are printed by the new 'fluo config' command (FluoConfig.main dumps getApplicationConfig()). Old code only stored fluo.app.* and fluo.tx.rollback.time.
FluoAdmin public interface gains two new abstract methods getConnectionConfig()/getApplicationConfig() with no default implementation — source/binary break for any external FluoAdmin implementer.

SimpleConfiguration.load(File/InputStream) made public, but both cast internalConfig to CompositeConfiguration; instances created via subset(prefix) (private Configuration constructor) hold a SubsetConfiguration, so calling load() on e.g. getAppConfiguration() throws ClassCastException.

FluoInit.main checks only args.length < 1 but unconditionally executes Arrays.copyOfRange(args, 3, args.length) and reads args[1]/args[2]; invoking with 1-2 args crashes with ArrayIndexOutOfBounds/IllegalArgumentException instead of printing usage.
FluoExec.main contains a dead statement 'Arrays.copyOfRange(args, 3, args.length);' whose result is discarded (duplicated correctly two lines later).

Environment.readZookeeperConfig: appConfig is now taken from the merged (local+ZK) config, so locally-passed fluo.app.* properties leak into appConfig — directly contradicting the retained comment 'make sure not to include config passed to env, only want config from zookeeper' (old code built appConfig from a ZK-only tmpConfig).

FluoGetJars catches all exceptions and only logs (exit code 0), so 'fluo oracle/worker' setup_service continues after a failed jar download; it also recursively deletes the user-supplied downloadPath if it exists (FileUtils.deleteDirectory) — dangerous with a wrong argument.

fluo script 'stop' sends SIGKILL (kill -s KILL) immediately to oracle/worker pids — no graceful SIGTERM shutdown for in-flight work; FluoOracle/FluoWorker mains also catch Exception, log, and fall through to exit code 0 on startup failure, and loop forever even if the service dies.

Unused imports/fields in new commands: FluoConfig and FluoGetProp import URI, FileUtils, hadoop Configuration/FileSystem/Path, slf4j (FluoGetProp has no logger; FluoConfig's log unused); FluoGetProp usage string says 'FluoGetProps'. Likely breaks checkstyle/import audits.
MiniFluoImpl now checks containsKey(ACCUMULO_TABLE_PROP) only; a user who set the deprecated fluo.admin.accumulo.table key (old fluo.properties) gets their table silently overridden to 'fluo'.

getDepPositiveInt() drops the 'value > 0' Preconditions check that getPositiveInt enforced, so getZookeeperTimeout() no longer validates positivity; validate() also doesn't cover new props (dfs root, observer init dir/jars url, accumulo jars).

FluoFactory.newAdmin javadoc lists 'fluo.accumulo.classpath' as a required property — no such property exists (deprecated key is fluo.admin.accumulo.classpath; replacement is fluo.accumulo.jars which has a default).

getJarsFromClasspath() casts the classloader to URLClassLoader — fragile (fails on Java 9+) and silently empty if jars don't match the regex; copyJarsToDfs does not clear the destination dir (stale jars accumulate across re-inits, unlike copyDirToDfs which deletes first).
New 'ps' command in the fluo script is not listed in print_usage; 'setup_service $2' in exec is unquoted; $app is unset for exec so its log dir resolves to $FLUO_LOG_DIR/.

guice 4.0 version is hardcoded in modules/command/pom.xml instead of parent dependencyManagement; hadoop classes (FileSystem, Path) used in FluoGetJars/FluoConfig rely on a transitive dependency via fluo-core.

qualitative: All three candidates reviewed essentially only the truncated (docs-only) portion of the diff plus the base repo, then asserted that the PR 'fails to update' things it actually updates: metrics.md was rewritten, SimpleConfiguration.load() was made public, fluo-conn/fluo-app.properties were added to the distribution, the old test was renamed and new multi-file tests added, and the CLIENT_* constants were deprecated. The recurring Oracle/WorkerRunnable and FluoInstall complaints are also false — those classes are part of the intentionally retained, @Deprecated old-fluo.properties path. The shared 'docs example is incomplete' claim misunderstands the PR's core design: clients now need only zookeepers + app name because accumulo credentials are merged from Zookeeper. v2 and v4 tie at -14 (v4 by saying less, v2 by hedging three points into harmless 'verify' statements); v3 lands at -15 despite being the only candidate with a genuinely good, verifiable point (boilerplate duplication across the ~11 new command mains), because it stacked the most fabricated claims. None found the real issues: plaintext Accumulo credentials now persisted to Zookeeper by updateSharedConfig, the FluoAdmin interface break, the public load() ClassCastException on subset configs, the FluoInit arg-handling crash, or the dead statement in FluoExec.
fabrication_pattern: Asserting changes are missing based on the truncated diff instead of treating unseen files as unknown.
